mini.explanation: A decision metric is a clearly defined number tied to a specific business choice.
mini.how: State the owner, formula, grain, refresh cadence, and action threshold before using it in a review.
mini.why: Shared vocabulary lets product, finance, and operations teams make decisions from the same facts.
mini.workplace: Weekly qualified-pipeline conversion by segment is used to decide where sales enablement should focus.
mistakes: Reporting a number without saying what decision it is meant to inform.
Treating a metric movement as causal before checking the business process behind it.

mini.explanation: Data provenance records where a dataset came from and how it was transformed.
mini.how: Track source systems, extraction time, transformations, joins, filters, and known exclusions.
mini.why: Shared vocabulary lets product, finance, and operations teams make decisions from the same facts.
mini.workplace: A dashboard footnote says revenue comes from booked invoices, excludes test accounts, and refreshes nightly.
mistakes: Trusting a polished dashboard without checking whether the data lineage matches the question.
Treating a metric movement as causal before checking the business process behind it.

mini.explanation: A validation split is a held-out portion of data used to estimate performance before final deployment.
mini.how: Separate training and validation records in a way that matches future use, especially for time-based data.
mini.why: Model quality depends on trustworthy data, appropriate validation, and clear interpretation.
mini.workplace: A churn model trains on January through September and validates on October accounts.
mistakes: Randomly splitting time-series data in a way that leaks future behavior into training.
Optimizing validation accuracy while ignoring whether the model can be acted on.

mini.explanation: Feature leakage happens when a model uses information that would not be available at prediction time.
mini.how: Review every feature against the exact moment the prediction would be made in production.
mini.why: Model quality depends on trustworthy data, appropriate validation, and clear interpretation.
mini.workplace: Using cancellation-date fields to predict cancellation risk creates artificially high validation scores.
mistakes: Celebrating high accuracy before auditing whether features are available at scoring time.
Optimizing validation accuracy while ignoring whether the model can be acted on.

mini.explanation: Metric grain is the level at which a metric is counted or aggregated.
mini.how: Declare whether each row represents an account, order, user, session, week, or other unit.
mini.why: Dashboards become decision systems only when metrics are consistently defined and easy to audit.
mini.workplace: Average revenue per account and average revenue per order answer different questions.
mistakes: Joining metrics with different grains and accidentally duplicating totals.
Building attractive charts before agreeing on the metric definition and grain.

mini.explanation: Filter context is the set of active filters that determines which records contribute to a result.
mini.how: Read slicers, page filters, visual filters, and formula-level filters before interpreting a number.
mini.why: Dashboards become decision systems only when metrics are consistently defined and easy to audit.
mini.workplace: A conversion rate changes when the dashboard is sliced to enterprise accounts only.
mistakes: Assuming a dashboard total and a filtered visual use the same denominator.
Building attractive charts before agreeing on the metric definition and grain.
